name: STO
description: Compact analog VCO. Triangle core with Sine, Variable Shape and Sub outputs. Linear FM, hard SYNC and gateable Sub-Oscillator. Designed as melodic counterpart to the DPO.

controls.name: FREQ (Coarse)
controls.description: Coarse tuning knob. Large grey knob.

controls.name: LIN
controls.description: Unipolar attenuator for Linear FM input.

controls.name: SHAPE
controls.description: Ripples even and odd harmonics while preserving fundamental. Acts as attenuator when SHAPE CV In is patched.

inputs.name: EXPO
inputs.description: Exponential frequency CV. No attenuator — patch a VCA inline for dynamic control.

inputs.name: LIN (FM)
inputs.description: AC-coupled Linear FM input. Level set by LIN knob.

inputs.name: SYNC
inputs.description: Hard sync. Resets oscillator core on rising edge of external VCO. Any 10Vpp audio-rate signal works.

inputs.name: SHAPE CV
inputs.description: Direct-coupled CV for Variable Shape. SHAPE knob becomes its attenuator when patched.

inputs.name: S-Gate
inputs.description: Turns Sub-Oscillator ON at gate high, OFF at gate low. Resets Sub on rising edge. Narrow audio signals can pseudo-sync Sub only.

outputs.name: Variable SHAPE
outputs.description: Triangle-core-derived output shaped by SHAPE. Carries FM/SYNC harmonics most aggressively.

outputs.name: SUB
outputs.description: Stepped rectangular sub-oscillator. One octave below Sine/Shape. Affected by EXPO, LIN FM and 1V/Oct; gated by S-Gate.

outputs.name: Sine
outputs.description: Sine wave derived from triangle core (same shaper circuit as DPO). Low harmonics — ideal FM carrier.

behaviors.name: Linear FM tracking limit
behaviors.description: Linear FM bus overdrives past 80% of LIN attenuator travel and the STO stops tracking 1V/Oct accurately. Keep LIN below 80% for pitched FM, push past for chaotic timbres that abandon pitch.

behaviors.name: Gateable Sub-Oscillator
behaviors.description: Sub output is ON only while S-Gate is high. Rising edge resets Sub. Gives Sub an independent rhythmic layer from Sine/Shape — patch a clock or rhythm gate here for a percussive sub-octave. Narrow audio signals at S-Gate produce sync-like harmonics only in Sub.

behaviors.name: Variable Shape morphing
behaviors.trigger: SHAPE knob or SHAPE CV In
behaviors.description: Ripples both even and odd harmonics while always preserving fundamental. 0%: pure sine. 100%: glitched triangle. Best driven by slow modulation (logarithmic envelopes, smooth random). SHAPE knob auto-converts from value to attenuator when SHAPE CV In is patched.

behaviors.name: Hard SYNC on triangle core
behaviors.description: STO core restarts on each rising edge of the external VCO. Base frequency locks to external VCO; harmonics emerge when STO frequency is higher than master. Sweep the STO FREQ via EXPO to animate SYNC harmonics. Best with master at 100Hz or higher.

behaviors.name: Expo FM via EXPO input
behaviors.description: No onboard attenuator — insert a VCA for dynamic/voltage-controlled index. Exponential FM is deeper than Linear but breaks 1V/Oct tracking. Use for percussion or chaos, not pitched leads.

behaviors.name: Sub as sub-harmonic layer
behaviors.trigger: monitor SUB directly or mix with Sine/Shape
behaviors.description: Sub is stepped rectangular, one octave down, tracking all pitch/FM/SYNC inputs. Combining SUB + Variable Shape (at an MMG or Optomix audio mixer) produces the thick melodic voice STO is designed for.

patchIdeas.name: Sub-Timbral melodic voice
patchIdeas.patch: Variable Shape → MMG/filter AC in · SUB → MMG DC in · STO FREQ ~12:00 · slow log LFO from MATHS → SHAPE CV, SHAPE knob ~60% · sequencer pitch → 1V/Oct · gate-triggered expo envelope → filter cutoff · rich sine + sub + animated harmonics under envelope control — the classic STO voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
STO:
* FREQ: ~12:00
* SHAPE: ~60% (as attenuator)

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> STO (1V/Oct)
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> STO (Shape CV) // slow log LFO
- STO (Sine) -> Filter (AC In)
- STO (SUB) -> Filter (DC In)
- Envelope (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Analog bass drum
patchIdeas.patch: Sine → Optomix CH 1 signal in · gate → STO Linear FM In AND Optomix CH 1 STRIKE · LIN ~12:00 · FREQ ~9:00 · Optomix DAMP and CONTROL full CCW · monitor Optomix CH 1 out · short FM burst on each gate gives analog kick. Modulate LIN amount or add Expo FM from another VCO for kick variants.
patchIdeas.diagram:
STO:
* FREQ: ~9:00
* LIN: ~12:00

- Gate Source (Gate) g> STO (LIN)
- Gate Source (Gate) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)
- STO (Sine) -> Optomix.CH 1 (Signal In)
- Optomix.CH 1 (Out) -> Output (In) // kick

patchIdeas.name: Transposition / superimposition
patchIdeas.patch: Main sequence → 1V/Oct · second sequence (odd clock division of master) → EXPO · the two sequences add for variable transposition. Alternatively derive transposer from main sequence via S&H for structural variation · or tune a square LFO to ±1V for double-octave leaps.
patchIdeas.diagram:
STO:
* FREQ: to taste

- Sequencer A (Pitch) p> STO (1V/Oct)
- Sequencer B (Pitch) p> STO (EXPO) // added — transposes main
- STO (Variable SHAPE) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual SYNC (split Sub from main)
patchIdeas.patch: Audio-rate square → S-Gate · different audio-rate square → SYNC · Sub pseudo-syncs to S-Gate source while Sine/Shape hard-sync to the SYNC source · gives two related-but-independent harmonic layers from one STO. Try DPO VCO A saw into S-Gate and VCO B square into SYNC with Follow engaged.
patchIdeas.diagram:
STO:
* FREQ: higher than sync sources

- VCO A (Square) -> STO (S-Gate) // pseudo-syncs Sub
- VCO B (Square) -> STO (SYNC) // hard-syncs Sine/Shape
- STO (Variable SHAPE) -> Mixer (In)
- STO (SUB) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Key-tracking Variable Shape
patchIdeas.patch: Mult 1V/Oct pitch CV to SHAPE CV In · Variable Shape → output · higher pitches get more harmonic content, mimicking filter key-tracking without a filter · pair with sequencer for a bright top-end that scales with pitch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
STO:
* SHAPE: ~50% (sets depth)

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out 1) p> STO (1V/Oct)
- Mult (Out 2) >> STO (Shape CV) // key-tracked harmonics
- STO (Variable SHAPE) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bassline with gated Sub
patchIdeas.patch: Slow square LFO (or cycling MATHS EOC) → S-Gate AND → sequencer clock · sequencer pitch → 1V/Oct · monitor SUB only · Sub turns on/off per clock step creating a note-like pulse without a VCA · adjust LFO pulse width (or MATHS Rise/Fall) to change note length.
patchIdeas.diagram:
STO:
* FREQ: bass range
* SHAPE: irrelevant

LFO:

- LFO (Square) c> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out 1) c> Sequencer (Clk)
- Mult (Out 2) g> STO (S-Gate) // gates Sub per step
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> STO (1V/Oct)
- STO (SUB) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dynamic Expo FM pings
patchIdeas.patch: Sine from another VCO → Optomix CH 1 · Optomix CH 1 out → STO EXPO · gate → Optomix STRIKE · Optomix CONTROL full CCW · each gate opens a short FM burst into EXPO · STO frequency disassembles for percussion accents that decay naturally with vactrol damping.
patchIdeas.diagram:
STO:
* FREQ: high register

VCO:

- VCO (Sine) -> Optomix.CH 1 (In)
- Gate Source (Gate) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)
- Optomix.CH 1 (Out) >> STO (EXPO) // dynamic FM
- STO (Variable SHAPE) -> Output (In) // percussive blip
